evals.prompt: Process 5,000 images through a Modal function that resizes them to thumbnails and collect all results
evals.expected_output: Working Python code using .map() or .starmap() to fan out 5,000 image paths/bytes across a Modal resize function, collect all thumbnail results in-process, and handle concurrency limits appropriately
expectations: The code uses .map() or .starmap() for synchronous fan-out and in-process result collection
The code does not use .spawn() since the caller needs results back immediately
The code includes a @app.function or @app.cls decorated resize function
The code demonstrates collecting all results (e.g., iterating the .map() generator or list(...))
The code sets or acknowledges max_containers or concurrency limits where relevant
The code uses a local_entrypoint or with app.run(): to orchestrate the 5,000 inputs

evals.prompt: I want to submit a long-running Modal job from my web server and let it run detached — the user should get a job ID they can poll later
evals.expected_output: Working Python code that uses .spawn() to submit a detached Modal job, returns a job ID (FunctionCall object or serialized handle), and shows how to poll or retrieve the result later without blocking the web server
expectations: The code uses .spawn() to submit the job asynchronously
The code captures and returns a job ID or FunctionCall handle to the caller
The code demonstrates how to poll or retrieve the result using the job ID (e.g., fc.get() or polling fc.get(timeout=0))
The code deploys the function with modal deploy or uses Function.from_name() so the web server can reference a stable remote function
The code does not block the web server request handler waiting for completion
The response explains the difference between .spawn() and .map() in this context

evals.prompt: I'm running Whisper transcription on Modal and want to batch multiple audio inputs together per GPU call to maximize utilization
evals.expected_output: Working Python code using @modal.batched to coalesce multiple Whisper transcription requests per GPU container call, with correct batch_size and wait_ms settings, and an explanation of the batching contract
expectations: The code uses @modal.batched decorator on the transcription function
The code sets a batch_size appropriate for GPU memory and throughput
The code sets wait_ms to control how long to wait for a batch to fill
The decorated function receives a list of inputs and returns a list of results (matching length)
The code uses GPU resources (gpu= parameter) on the batched function
The response explains or demonstrates how callers invoke the batched function (individual calls that get coalesced)
